title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes:
Updating report from Bronze to Silver.

1. Keyboard still goes missing from time to time but only after using a text dialog (cat, renaming builds, etc), might not be a problem for those who play with Controller.  Workaround - Use the text dialogs as little as possible.

2. In Game Tutorial still crash the game.  Workaround - Don't view them.  Besides, there are better videos on Youtube.

3. Game freezes video card.  Workaround - None.  The way to mitigate this is to have an SSH session into the box to kill MonsterHunter processes.  Once the process is killed, GUI unfreezes and you can restart.  I've seen people say they can CTRL-ALT-F2 to a console session and kill from there.  Has not worked for me.

4. To minimize lost play, save often!  Every time you change inventory or interact with the game's various systems (Bounties, botanical, Argosy, etc) the changes will not be retained until you save.

The reason why I am bumping this up to Silver is because the only really major issue is the graphics freezing and there's a mitigation for that.  So much so I'm 52 hours in according to Steam, ~46 according to the game do to lost progress from crashes.  That, to me, fits "Generally playable."

title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes:
On default ubuntu setup, the game crashes on startup. On inspecting the log file following the instruction here https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/175#issuecomment-439889495 the error similar to this one here https://forums.lutris.net/t/solved-unsupported-graphics-card-linux-vulkan/4408 which can be solved by installing a few packages https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/wiki/Common-issues#vulkan-errors After that the game runs.␣␣␣␣␣


Over the seven hours of playthrough, the keyboard became unresponsive while the mouse still working, twice. I recommend that you run the game in borderless so at least you could kill the game from the terminal and restart. When in full screen alt-tab will only bring you right back to the game. I had to resolve it by shutting the system down. ␣␣␣␣␣


The game runs ok. There is a low of micro stuttering on my system despite running 90 fps on low setting. The frame time jumps between 10ms to 40ms with spikes reaching 200ms. This introduces a lot of input lag ( or perhaps input drop ) that make the game pretty unplayable in certain areas. After running different setting, it seems that the input lag exists regardless of the graphics setting. The game has not crashed on me during normal playthoughs, but watching the ingame video tutorials WILL CRASH.  ␣␣␣␣␣


The cpu is always hover around 50% utilization, looks like half the threads are not used. I have not run the game on windows, but watching videos with systems that are less powerful, this is likely a linux issue. ␣␣␣␣␣


You can enable DXVK_HUD by plugging this into the launch options and watch those beautiful spikes: DXVK_HUD=fps,frametimes,submissions,drawcalls,pipelines,memory %command% All in all, for a windows only game, this runs way better than my expectations. ␣␣␣␣␣


Checkout the issue thread on github for latest problems and solutions: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/175
title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes: Judging from some commentary here, I was expecting a much greater performance impact in comparison to Windows. However, I managed to get a solid 60 fps on my RX 580 by just setting graphics to High, disabling Volume Rendering and SH Diffuse to Low; both High Resolution Scaling and HD textures are active, and you can even throw FXAA + TAA with no fuss. It's a CPU heavy application though, and it can easily bottleneck by just keeping your browser open, so make sure to close everything before running. Apart from that, I also stumbled upon the controller problem, but that's also on Windows and seems to be a Linux issue, not a Proton one. Overall, a very nice result for a heavy game released last year.

title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes:
Able to get it working now on Ryzen 3000 with the tweaks discovered in the github issue: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2927 
Add clearcpuid=514 as a kernel boot parameter (https://askubuntu.com/questions/19486/how-do-i-add-a-kernel-boot-parameter)
title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes: when using a zen 2 processor, you need to add clearcpuid=514 to your kernel boot options, this disables UMIP support. Once I got that disabled, everything worked fine, but it's pretty laggy. You can learn to add a kernel boot parameter here: https://askubuntu.com/questions/19486/how-do-i-add-a-kernel-boot-parameter

title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes:
This is how I had to install the "https://github.com/z0z0z/mf-install" to get the game to run and not crash. Aftrer downloading the mf-install-master.zip you will need to extract the zip. Just right click and select extract on the file. I had to find the location of the game installation. In the file manager search 582010 to find it. Then just open the terminal and just follow what I did, just make sure you put the correct location for the game installation in the last command.

aj@pop-os:~$ cd Downloads
aj@pop-os:~/Downloads$ cd mf-install-master
aj@pop-os:~/Downloads/mf-install-master$ WINEPREFIX="/home/aj/.steam/debian-installation/steamapps/compatdata/582010/pfx" ./install-mf.sh

Hope this helps some.
Enjoy!
title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes: played over 100 hours. only tutorial vids crashed the game. but this can be worked out by using z0z0z git. Just install wine. download and  extract the folder. go to  a terminal and go into that  folder and fire up this command: WINEPREFIX="/home/youruser/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/582010/pfx" ./install-ms.sh

title: MONSTER HUNTER: WORLD

notes:
Fist of all if you are usin a zen 2 procesor like the one I have 3700x ect, make sure to boot with the kernel parameter clearcpuid=514 or the game wont run, if you want the cutscene play and be able to finish the game and continue, you have to install https://github.com/z0z0z/mf-install workaround, also very importan you have to have the codecs installed to be able to play mp4 other wise it wont work. ok POP_OS! 19.04  I did this :
sudo apt install -y gstreamer1.0-plugins-bad gstreamer1.0-plugins-ugly gstreamer1.0-plugins-good libavcodec-extra gstreamer1.0-libav chromium-codecs-ffmpeg-extra libdvd-pkg

sudo apt install ubuntu-restricted-extras

that is it enjoy.

notes: I had some troubles with game crashing which i seems solved by Nvidia testing drivers. Currently it's working fine, with stable 60 fps with v-sync on max details, except some shuttering during heavy action (FPS counter shows up stable 60, but it feels like 15-20). I also have some problems with switching to second screen during gameplay (minor issues for most of players) - it's very annoying, but i found some, not so bad workaround - "ALT + Enter" to switch to windowed mode, then "ALT + Tab" to switch to another process. Borderless window is not an option it always shows up on left screen.
